channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Why is Iran increasingly targeting the UAE in its war messaging?
item.description: The UAE's close ties to the US and Israel have placed the Gulf country under Iranian suspicion.

item.title: Ukraine hits Russia’s distant gas facilities after Moscow’s attacks kill 6
item.description: The attack comes in retaliation for the Russian attacks that killed at least six in Ukraine, according to the president.

item.title: Moment taxi driver wrestles armed man during police chase in Turkey
item.description: A taxi driver wrestled a gunman that was forcing him to drive during a police chase in Turkey.

item.title: Macron unveils $27 billion Africa investment, urges reset with Europe
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron, in Kenya for the Africa Forward summit, called for a ‘fundamental reset’ in Africa.

item.title: Trump skirts question on US ‘red lines’ for Iran ceasefire
item.description: Trump skirted a question from a reporter about whether he has a ‘red line’ that would end the ceasefire with Iran.

item.title: ‘Cotton picking’: US lawmaker condemned for racist comment about Jeffries
item.description: US representative Jen Kiggans appears to endorse racist statements about top House Democrat Hakeem Jeffries.

item.title: Kevin Warsh confirmed to US Federal Reserve board in close Senate vote
item.description: Senator John Fetterman of Pennsylvania, a Democrat, joins Republicans to advance Warsh's confirmation.

item.title: Serbia hosts first joint military exercise with NATO
item.description: The two-week-long drills come less than 30 years after NATO bombed Belgrade in the Kosovo war.

item.title: Voters head to the polls in the Bahamas for high-stakes snap election
item.description: Affordability, wage growth, and housing costs dominate Bahamian snap election as parties vie for 41 parliamentary seats.

item.title: Israel sent Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to UAE: US envoy
item.description: Mike Huckabee calls on Gulf states to 'pick a side' in the war between Israel and Iran as he heaps praise on Abu Dhabi.

item.title: US faces rising costs with Iran war driving energy prices, inflation higher
item.description: Consumer prices spike in April, driven by soaring energy costs, with petrol prices up nearly 30 percent since last year.

item.title: Inside US Democrats’ Gaza fight – and what it means for 2028 elections
item.description: Support for Israel was once untouchable in US politics. Now Democrats are divided. Is this a turning point for the party

item.title: EBay rejects GameStop’s $56bn bid as ‘neither credible nor attractive’
item.description: CEO Cohen, who has built a 5 percent position in eBay, has hinted he may take offer directly to eBay shareholders.

item.title: Can China counter US sanctions with its trade power?
item.description: From sanctions to rare earths, the economic rivalry between the US and China is intensifying.

item.title: Israel’s war creating a ‘lost generation’ of Lebanese students
item.description: Israel's war on Lebanon has displaced hundreds of thousands, and is the latest crisis facing country's education system.

item.title: Kuwait says it foils infiltration operation by Iran’s IRGC
item.description: Kuwait arrests four men it accuses of being in Revolutionary Guard and conducting failed infiltration of Bubiyan Island.

item.title: Pentagon gives new $29bn Iran war price tag, downplays munitions concerns
item.description: Appearing before congressional panel, Defense Secretary Hegseth says US is prepared to either escalate or wind down war.

item.title: Israel kills, injures average of 4 children daily amid Lebanon ‘ceasefire’
item.description: The statistics shared by Save the Children refer to the first 25 days of the truce that came into effect on April 16.

item.title: What to know about Curacao at the FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: After a fairytale qualification journey, the tiny Caribbean island makes its World Cup debut next month.

item.title: Israel killing at least one child a week in occupied West Bank, UN says
item.description: Israeli military and settlers have killed at least one Palestinian child a week on average in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: How one Palestinian teen’s life changed forever after Israeli gunfire
item.description: Amir Othman was 16 when Israeli soldiers shot him on a hill near Nablus in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: Lamine Yamal’s Palestine flag wave hailed by fans, activists and athletes
item.description: Palestinians in Gaza, football fans from around the world and activists laud Barca and Spain footballer.

item.title: The Flotilla Diaries: Inside the 2025 mission to break the Gaza siege
item.description: Determined to break the siege on Gaza, two filmmakers share firsthand accounts on board the Global Sumud Flotilla.

item.title: Rana stars as Bangladesh win first home Test against Pakistan
item.description: Five second-innings wickets by Nahid ‌Rana allow Bangladesh to claim a maiden home Test victory against Pakistan.

item.title: Trump-Xi meeting: Could China, US form a ‘G2’?
item.description: The leaders' upcoming meeting in Beijing has revived the idea of a 'Group of Two' between the superpowers.
